mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.14% as of April 2012), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over quarterly horizons as investors reach for yield in risk assets.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX 60-day returns during sub-0.25% FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals improving labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=8.1% vintage_date=2012-04-01, down from prior quarters
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of cases, or the effect disappears (p > 0.10) when tested against a matched non-triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in INDPRO and PAYEMS growth rates signals a synchronized expansion in industrial output and hiring that is not captured by either series alone, reinforcing risk-on positioning and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month; the interaction term captures confirmation effects absent when only one series improves.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND PAYEMS 3-month average change > 150k
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows no statistically significant improvement in mean 20-day forward return versus INDPRO-only or PAYEMS-only triggers (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10 in regression).

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses above a rolling 12-month moving average after a prolonged trough, it signals a durable turn in residential construction demand that leads broader economic activity and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its trailing 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=654-695k vintage_date=2012-03-01, recovering from post-crisis lows
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following HOUST crossovers are indistinguishable from unconditional base-rate returns (difference in means not significant at p<0.10) across at least two independent 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime, rising CPIAUCSL combined with unchanged near-zero FEDFUNDS widens the implied real-rate gap, initially interpreted by markets as supportive of risk assets because policy tightening is precluded by the ZLB constraint, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; this relationship inverts once FEDFUNDS begins normalizing.
failure_condition: Once FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% in any subsequent period, the hypothesis is retested and rejected if the positive relationship persists (sign does not flip) rather than reversing to negative or neutral.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending, which mechanically feeds into near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX return remains below 0.02 across the full 2005-2012 sample, or sign of correlation flips in more than 40% of rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A PAYEMS print that surprises meaningfully above the trailing 6-month average signals stronger-than-expected labor demand, triggering a short-lived positive repricing of equities within the first week of trading following the release as risk appetite improves.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change exceeds trailing 6-month average by more than 50k
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return following triggered PAYEMS surprises is not significantly different from the unconditional 5-day mean return (p > 0.10) over a rolling 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime, sustained positive GDPC1 growth combined with rising INDPRO confirms broadening real output expansion beyond a single sector, and equities re-rate upward over a quarterly horizon as earnings growth expectations extend; this mechanism weakens or reverses once the expansion matures into a late-cycle regime with decelerating INDPRO.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage_value=13502.4 vs revised_value=17367.01 indicating high revision risk
failure_condition: If the joint-trigger sample fails to outperform the GDPC1-only trigger sample by a statistically significant margin (p>0.10), or if the relationship reverses sign once INDPRO growth decelerates below 0% in later-cycle periods without hypothesis adjustment, the hypothesis is rejected.
